prompt:
Use case: historical-scene
Asset type: 2×1 close-up action-parts sheet for scene 4 of a layered Chinese paper-collage animation
Input images: Image 1 is the approved creek style sample; match the elderly woman's muted ochre sleeves, aged hands, thick iron pestle and exact handmade paper language
Primary request: two complete isolated close-up action phases, one per horizontal cell. In each cell, both elderly hands grip the upper and middle part of ONE thick iron pestle. The shaft slopes downward at a shallow 20–30 degree diagonal. ONLY THE LOWER END touches a small flat grindstone patch at the bottom of the cutout; every other part of the shaft is visibly raised. Cell 2 advances the hands and pestle a short distance ALONG THE SHAFT AXIS, showing the next phase of a lengthwise push-pull stroke while the same tip remains the sole abrasion point. Show a subtle flattened-to-tapered wear shape at that contacted tip, still much thicker than a needle.
Action mechanics: axial back-and-forth rubbing of one end against stone to make that end gradually pointed. The hands do not roll the pestle around its circumference.
Style/medium: premium handcrafted Chinese paper collage, fibrous torn edges, restrained ink, matte iron gray and ochre mineral pigments
Composition/framing: exact 2 columns × 1 row, consistent hand anatomy and pestle thickness, generous padding, no overlap, complete wrists and fingers; keep the tip-to-stone contact readable
Scene/backdrop: perfectly flat solid #ff00ff chroma-key background in both cells, no floor, cast shadow, gradient, texture or divider outside the small stone contact patch
Constraints: the rod must not lie flat; only one end touches stone; no finished needle, sparks, text, label, watermark, logo, extra limbs or #ff00ff in subject
Avoid: horizontal rod resting across the stone, one hand at each rod end, sideways rolling, polishing the full cylindrical surface, sawing motion, distorted fingers, warped pestle, photorealism, glossy 3D, cropped hands, motion blur
